body.title: Build audit-design skill — called by /decided
body.result: Built ~/.claude/skills/audit-design/SKILL.md with 9 positive checks (approach-frame goal, runtime-observable success, alternatives, constraints, missing-pass, 30d conflicts, palace-rule conflicts, decomposition, executor+inertia). PASS/AMEND verdict; AMEND blocks /decided Step 3. HIGH-inertia file mentions surface inline as separate gate. Standalone /audit-design <D-id> supported. /decided Step 2.5 inserted to invoke the audit. Model: opus. Telemetry emission described, waits on T-audit-telemetry-shape. Pushed in ac8ea17b.
body.description:
New skill audit-design — invoked by /decided before drafting tickets. Catches a 'decision' that isn't actually decided. Checks: goal stated as positive target (approach-frame); success condition observable in runtime not just code; alternatives + why this one named; constraints named (HIGH-inertia files, gates, dependencies); 'what am I missing / what could be better' answered; conflicts with last-30d decisions; palace-rule conflicts (Postgres-only, oop-first, docs-in-code, no-new-memory-schemas, no-speculative-flags, inherit-base-class); scope decomposed into atomic ticketable units; per-piece executor + inertia tier named.

Return AMEND when checks fail. Emits per-run record via audit_telemetry.

Model: Opus (architecture judgment is exactly Sonnet's weak spot).

**Affected files:** new ~/.claude/skills/audit-design/SKILL.md, /decided skill updated to invoke audit-design before step 3.
**Design rules:** no persistence beyond palace telemetry. /decided invocation is the integration point.
**Test plan:** seed a deliberately-vague decision, invoke /decided → audit-design returns AMEND with specific failed checks. Seed a clean decision → PASS. Telemetry record written to palace.
